reasoning_model_1: The model generates the audio with accurate content and high fluency. However, regarding the core paralinguistic instruction to 'start with extremely slow speed, then gradually increase to medium speed', the model fails significantly. The audio maintains a consistent, moderate-to-fast speaking rate from the very beginning ('清晨的阳光...'). There is no perceptible 'extremely slow' phase at the start (0:00-0:03), and consequently, no gradual acceleration is observed throughout the clip. The speed remains uniform, ignoring the dynamic adjustment command entirely.
reasoning_model_2: The model follows the instructions effectively. The audio begins with a distinctly slow pace, characterized by pauses and deliberate enunciation in the first segment ('清晨的阳光，缓缓洒落在窗边' ~0:00-0:06). As the script progresses to '嗯，该起床了', the pacing begins to shift. Finally, in the last segment ('得赶紧去准备早餐了'), the speed noticeably increases to a natural medium-fast flow, conveying the sense of urgency mentioned in the text ('赶紧'). The dynamic adjustment from extremely slow to medium speed is smooth and clearly discernible, matching the user's requirements perfectly.
model_comparison: Model 1 failed to implement the requested speed changes, delivering the entire script at a constant, standard pace. It completely ignored the 'extremely slow start' and 'gradual increase' constraints. Model 2, in contrast, demonstrated excellent control over the paralinguistic feature of speed (语速). It correctly started with a very slow, measured pace suitable for the 'morning sunlight' description and effectively ramped up the tempo to match the 'hurry' context at the end. Model 2 successfully achieved the dynamic adjustment required by the prompt, whereas Model 1 did not.
